category_title: Students with complex learning and behavioral disabilities (developmental delay)
minimal_context: Dang is working on single-digit addition problems during mathematics class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for mathematics. Please solve these single-digit addition problems on your worksheet.

STUDENT: (Looks at the math exercises and appears anxious) I can't do it. I don't know the answer.

TUTOR: What do you think the answer is?

STUDENT: (Looks ashamed of herself for not performing well) It's wrong. I'm doing it wrong.

TUTOR: Keep trying to finish the page.

STUDENT: (Becomes unhappy, disorderly, and unsettled, pushing the math materials away) I don't want to do it anymore!

options.A: Incorporate relevant mathematical objectives into a checklist that can be monitored for the student during the intervention period.
options.B: Learn details of the student's life outside of school by having lunch with her periodically, visiting her family, or participating in the community.
options.C: Develop the tutor's stimulus control to manage the student and the classroom contextual events during the intervention period.
options.D: Create pictorial descriptions of the single-digit addition problems for the student to use during the intervention period.
